calendar.matters:
Petition For Settlement Of Accounts And Approval Of Acts And Transactions Of Co-Trustees For 2014 And 2015 (Probate Code Sec. 17200 (B)(5)
Off Calendar Oct-06-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Christopher Jew for (petitioner & trustee) Daniel Rosenthal.

The Court stated that it does not have time sheets to support the appropriateness of the fees requested, that the wrong formula was used and that the guideline was not relied upon. Mr. Jew stated that the parties did not record time and that they will have to submit on the declarations provided. The Court stated that the result will be allowing fees at the 1% level but subtracting fees paid for the accounting, that it will need a new order restoring the fees overpaid and stated that the 1% fee is $54,343.00 subtracting $19,704.00 for the accounting preparation which leaves a balance of $34,639.00 with $28,000.00 due to restore. Mr. Jew stated that he will submit a revised order. The Court took the Petition for Settlement of Accounts and Approval of Acts and Transactions of Co-Trustees for 2014 & 2015 under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

Petition For Settlement Of Accounts And Approval Of Acts And Transactions Of Co-Trustees For 2014 And 2015 (Probate Code Sec. 17200 (B)(5)
Off Calendar Aug-30-2016 Continued To Sep-20-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Christopher Jew for petitioners/trustees.

The Court expressed concern regarding the requested trustees fees, noting that the trustees were seeking payment of statutory fees premitted under the code for estate administration but not for trust admainistration. Mr. Jew responded that the fees requested worked out to less than 1% of the assets being administered, which he said was fair for the work performed even if the correct methodology had not been used. He stated that it was a complicated trust administration, and that the trustees had wanted to make distributions to the beneficiaries as quickly as possible. He stated that disbursements of stock were made on a division rate which corresponded to that set in the trust (70%-20%-10%), and the bonds could not be disbursed at the same time so that a minor variance from the original valuation had occurred.
The Court stated the further cocern that the request for extraordinary fees was not supported by submission of time sheets. Mr. Jew stated that he would like to file the time sheets, and requested a brief continuance to permit their filing. The matter was continued to September 20, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not reported)
